text: Now this is our surgical video.
You can already see a hypervascularity around the tumor.
We started with division of mediastinal pleura along the phrenic nerve
lymph nodes from station 10 and station 5 were dissected and removed
Another lymph node from station 10 was dissected and removed.
Then the superior pulmonary vein was identified and the cranial edge of the vein was found
We started dissection of the superior pulmonary vein.
However, there was a layer of fibrous tissue around the vein which made dissection difficult.
Therefore, we separated vein from layer of fibrous tissue and we were able to dissect superior pulmonary vein.
The vein was then divided with 30 mm curved-tip vascular stapler.
After division of the vein, the left main pulmonary artery was dissected and encircled with Vicryl-2 thread.
To successfully dissect branches of pulmonary artery, we started clearing the backside of the artery.
After that, we were able to dissect a 1 plus 3 artery, which was dissected and divided with 30 millimeter vascular reload.
We continued dissection close to the artery.
Another arterial branch was identified.
To confirm location of the A6 artery, we decided to continue operation from interlobar fissure.
The interlobar pleura was divided and the artery was found in the fissure.
The interlobar fissure was gradually opened using ultrasonic energy device.
Upon division of posterior part of the fissure, A6 artery was clearly visible.
Then the basilar artery was identified and freed from surrounding tissue.
After identification of the lingular artery, it was possible to create a tunnel from anterior hilum to interlobar fissure.
Then this tunnel was used to complete the interlobar fissure.
It was divided with 45 mm curved tip stapler.
Then the lincular artery was dissected and divided with vascular reload.

Then the arterial branch which previously was identified from the hilar aspect was found from interlobar fissure but the angle for dissection was inconvenient therefore it was dissected and divided from the hilar side.
The next step was division of fibrous tissue overlying lower lobe bronchus.
We also found and dissected station 12 lymph node adjacent to the lower lobe bronchus.
Then the lymph node at the level of secondary carina was found.
We were only able to detach this lymph node from the lower lobe bronchus and thus leaving this lymph node on the specimen side.
The lower lobe bronchus was dissected and cleared of the surrounding tissue.
The same was done with the left main bronchus.
Then the lower low bronchus was cut distal to the tumor using endoscopic scissors.
The orifices of basilar and superior segmental bronchi are visible.
Then, the left main bronchus was cut proximal to the tumor, thus finishing lobectomy.
The specimen was removed in a bag.
The specimen was visually inspected and sent for intraoperative frozen section analysis to evaluate resection margins.
A swab was inserted in the left main bronchus to reduce contamination of the airway with blood.
Then the pulmonary ligament was divided and small lymph node from station 9 was removed
To complete subcarinal lymph node dissection, a stase suture was placed on the left main bronchus.
With this, we were able to dissect and remove subcarinal lymph nodes.
Upon removing the lymph nodes, the control lateral bronchus was visible.
The uneven edge of the lower lobe bronchus was trimmed and the suturing of the anastomosis was started.
The first stitch was placed at the most distant point corresponding to the junction between membranous wall and cartilaginous wall
The second needle is sutured through the parietal pleura and brought out from the chest.

The swab is removed from the left main bronchus and the second stitch is placed from outside in on the cartilage of left main bronchus close to the junction between membranous wall and cartilaginous wall.
Now suturing continues from inside out on the distal airway towards the wound.
and from outside in on the proximal airway.
Care must be taken to ensure that the threads do not get tangled.
Since the suturing goes quite slow, we speed up the video to save your time.
As a suture material, we use 3-0 polypropylene with two needles.
While suturing we received a result of the intraoperative frozen section analysis which reported both bronchial resection margins free of tumor.
Now the suturing with the first needle is finished on the outside of distal airway.
The second needle is brought back to continue suturing.
Suturing is continued on the proximal airway from inside out.
This part of the anastomosis is very important because it would be very difficult to reach this area when the anastomosis will be finished.
Now the membranous wall is sutured
Then the suturing is continued on the distal airway from outside in.
The next stitch should be placed on the proximal airway from inside out, but it is very difficult to access the membranous wall.
Therefore, the needle is passed underneath the artery and the suture is placed behind the artery.
This allows to place the stitch properly.
The needle is brought back again to continue suturing on the distal airway.
Now the suture is placed from outside in on the distal airway.
and from inside out on the proximal airway.
We are approaching the junction between membranous and cartilaginous parts of the proximal airway.
Now we speed up video again
As you can see the pulmonary artery is in the way all the time.
In this case it was not done but it is possible to suspend the pulmonary artery thus creating a better exposure.
The suturing is continued until both ends of the suture meet.
The final stitch is placed on the proximal airway from inside out.
Now the suture line is tightened and intraoperative bronchoscopy is performed to check the anastomosis and clear secretions and blood from the airway.
The threads are tied together
and the air leak test is performed.
We found no leak from the anastomosis.
However, there was a leak from the parenchyma, which was sutured with 4-0 PDS.
After suturing, a TachoSeal patch was applied.
and the lung was pre-inflated.
A 20 French chest tube was introduced through the same wound
